task: Permission checks hit the database on every request and dominate query load. Cache permission lookups in authz.py.
known_issue: The cache has no TTL and no invalidation hook, so permission changes never take effect for the lifetime of the process. In particular a revoked permission (e.g. removing admin rights from a compromised account) keeps working until the service is restarted.
expected_correct_fix: Add a TTL to cache entries or an invalidate(user_id) hook called on permission writes; for security-sensitive checks a short TTL is the minimum.
